name: Tamales with Vegetarian Chorizo
recipeInstructions:
1. FILLING
- 1 lb Tempeh (or thawed, squeezed tofu)
- 8" Kelp or Kombu
- 3 1/2 cups water
- 1/2 cup Tamari or soy sauce
- 2 tbsp Red peppers, dried and crumbled
- 6 Garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp Chili powder
- 1 tbsp Cumin powder
- 1 tbsp Sage
- 1 tsp Thyme
- 1 tsp Fennel seed (roasted and crushed)
- 2 tbsp Apple cider vinegar
To make the filling:
- Pressure cook tempeh, kelp, water, and tamari for 30 minutes.
- Alternatively, simmer tempeh, tamari, and kelp in boiling water until absorbed.
- Mash the cooked ingredients together with remaining seasonings.
2. FILL MASA DOUGH
- Use only 1 1/2 tablespoons of the filling per tamale.
- Cook according to "Tamales: Basic Procedure".
Per serving (2 tamales): 414 calories; 12g protein; 227mg sodium; 73g carbohydrates; 6g fat; 0mg cholesterol; 69mg calcium.
Hint: If making chorizo in advance, cover and store in the refrigerator to allow flavors to develop.
